# Description

<!-- What does this PR do and why? Link any related issue with "Closes #123". -->

## Type of change

<!-- Use a Conventional Commit type in your PR title (e.g. "feat: ...", "fix: ...").
     The type drives automatic versioning, so pick the one that fits. -->

- [ ] `feat` — new feature (minor version bump)
- [ ] `fix` — bug fix (patch version bump)
- [ ] `docs` — documentation only
- [ ] `refactor` — code change that neither fixes a bug nor adds a feature
- [ ] `chore` — tooling, config, dependencies
- [ ] Breaking change (`!` / `BREAKING CHANGE:` — major version bump)

## Checklist

- [ ] Commits follow [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/)
- [ ] `npm run lint` passes
- [ ] `npm run format` passes
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` passes
- [ ] I tested the change on a device/emulator
